actions.description:
Actions that will be displayed to the right of the content
Wraps and stacks actions under content when alert's size is <=600px
eg
```
<Alert
 title='Title'
 actions={<Button onClick={doSomething}>Yes</Button>}
/>
```

stories.name: Round
stories.snippet:
const Round = () => <AvatarComponent
    name="Bob"
    size="64px"
    id="@bob:example.org"
    src="/images/__test__/kitten.jpg"
    type="round" />;

    name="Bob"
    size="64px"

    src="/images/__test__/kitten.jpg"
    type="square" />;

stories.name: Button

const Button = () => <AvatarComponent
    name="Bob"
    size="64px"
    id="@bob:example.org"
    src="/images/__test__/kitten.jpg"
    type="round"
    onClick={() => console.log("clicked!")} />;

    <AvatarComponent name="Bob" size="36px" src="" type="round" id="1" />
    <AvatarComponent name="Bob" size="36px" src="" type="round" id="2" />

    <AvatarComponent name="Bob" size="36px" src="" type="round" id="5" />
    <AvatarComponent name="Bob" size="36px" src="" type="round" id="6" />
</>;

avatar-avatarstack.name: AvatarStackComponent

stories.name: Avatar Stack
stories.snippet:
const AvatarStack = () => <AvatarStackComponent>(<>
      <Avatar
        name="Bob"
        id="@bob:example.org"
        size="20px"
        src="/images/__test__/kitten2.jpg"
      />
      <Avatar
        name="Alice"
        id="@alice:example.org"
        size="20px"
        src="/images/__test__/kitten.jpg"
      />
      <Avatar
        name="Bob"
        id="@bob:example.org"
        size="20px"
        src="/images/__test__/kitten2.jpg"
      />
    </>)</AvatarStackComponent>;

avatar-avatarstack.import: import { Avatar, AvatarStack as AvatarStackComponent } from "@vector-im/compound-web";
avatar-avatarstack.description: Renders a stack of avatars and clips the content appropriately. The `type` of avatars should always be set to `round` And all the avatars should have the same size.

reactDocgenTypescript.description:
Renders a stack of avatars and clips the content appropriately.

The `type` of avatars should always be set to `round`
And all the avatars should have the same size.

button-iconbutton.description: Display an icon as a button. Can render an indicator

reactDocgenTypescript.description: Display an icon as a button. Can render an indicator

disabled.description:
Note that disabled attribute is not added to buttons, so that disabled buttons are discoverable by keyboard.
`aria-disabled` attribute is used to indicate button is disabled.
Event handlers are not passed to disabled buttons (onClick, onSubmit, etc.)
Whether the button is interactable
disabled.name: disabled

destructive.description: Whether this button triggers a destructive action.
destructive.name: destructive

size.description:
The size of the button in CSS units, e.g. `"24px"`.
Note that this is the size of the *button* itself: the icon will be 0.75 * this size
size.name: size

indicator.description:
The icon button indicator dot displayed on the top right
As in IndicatorIcon
indicator.name: indicator

noBackground.description: Hide the background when the button is not active or hovered.
noBackground.name: noBackground

form-controls-editinplace.description: A text box with save/cancel buttons that appear when the field is active. Since thios control has its own 'save' button, it should *not* appear as part of a larger form: it exists as its own form that submits separately.

reactDocgenTypescript.description:
A text box with save/cancel buttons that appear when the field is active.
Since thios control has its own 'save' button, it should *not* appear as part
of a larger form: it exists as its own form that submits separately.

menu-menuitem.description: An item within a menu, acting either as a navigation button, or simply a container for other interactive elements. Must be used within a compound Menu or other `menu` or `menubar` aria role subtree.

reactDocgenTypescript.description:
An item within a menu, acting either as a navigation button, or simply a
container for other interactive elements.
Must be used within a compound Menu or other `menu` or `menubar` aria role subtree.

as.description: The element type of this menu item.
as.name: as

Icon.description:
The icon to show on this menu item.
When `Icon` is a ReactElement, it should spread the props
Icon.name: Icon

label.description: The label to show on this menu item.
label.name: label

stories.name: Tab Role

aria-label.description: Require a label for navigation landmarks
aria-label.name: aria-label

role.description:
Accessibility role that defaults to navigation.

If you pass tablist you must also pass `aria-controls` as prop to your NavItem and must
ensure that the conditions stated in https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/tabs/#wai-ariaroles,states,andproperties
are met.
role.name: role

tint.description:
The colour variant to use for the progress bar indicator

A function to get the text label to display in the progress bar.
If set, it will display a label on top of the progress bar

Default
With String Caption

tooltip.import:
import { IconButton, Tooltip as TooltipComponent, TooltipProvider } from "@vector-im/compound-web";
import UserIcon from "@vector-im/compound-design-tokens/assets/web/icons/user-profile";

disabled.description: Whether the tooltip should be forced to be in a closed state.
disabled.name: disabled

caption.description:
The caption of the tooltip.
JSX.Element can be used to provide accessibility content like kbd element.
Keep in mind, the caption should not be used for interactive content.
caption.name: caption

aria-atomic.description:
Additional aria-* attributes to pass through to the floating tooltip for
edge cases which require more user awareness like errors & alerts.
aria-atomic.name: aria-atomic

open.description:
The controlled open state of the tooltip.
If provided, the tooltip will be in controlled mode.
When true, the tooltip is always open. When false, the tooltip is always hidden.
When undefined, the tooltip will manage its own open state.
You will mostly want to omit this property. Will be used the vast majority
of the time during development.
open.name: open

onOpenChange.description: The event handler for the open change.
onOpenChange.name: onOpenChange

type.name: ((open: boolean, event?: Event, reason?: OpenChangeReason) => void) | undefined

label.description: A label for the target element.
label.name: label

isTriggerInteractive.description:
Whether the trigger element is interactive.
When trigger is interactive:
     - tooltip will be shown after a 300ms delay.
When trigger is not interactive:
     - tooltip will be shown instantly when pointer enters trigger.
     - trigger will be wrapped in a span with a tab index from prop nonInteractiveTriggerTabIndex
isTriggerInteractive.name: isTriggerInteractive

nonInteractiveTriggerTabIndex.description: The tab index for the non interactive trigger.
nonInteractiveTriggerTabIndex.name: nonInteractiveTriggerTabIndex

description.description: A description for the target element.
description.name: description
